title: Comparing four prompt strategies for legal document review (2-week eval)
body_excerpt: Over two weeks we tested four prompt strategies on a corpus of 1,200 contract clauses for substantive review. Strategy A (3-shot with reasoning trace) hit 91% precision; Strategy B (0-shot with structured output schema) hit 94%; Strategy C (RAG with BM25 + dense rerank over a clause-precedent index) hit 96% but failed catastrophically on novel contract types — all 7 missed clauses came from the IP-license category we hadn't seen at indexing time. Strategy D... [continues with full mechanism, prompt text, and a side-by-side table]
expected_reason: Quantified outcome over 2 weeks (evidence_quality 4–5), four named strategies with specifics (mechanism_specificity 4–5), failure mode disclosed (mechanism_specificity 5), useful for any retrieval-heavy workflow not just legal (cross-domain transfer; diversity_bonus 3).
